epics.epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Enrich patient demographics contract: multi-identifier-type, address hierarchy, person/contact split (contract+SDK first)
items.person_centric: Demographics become a person-owned record. The contract gains the fields a self-registration journey needs (identifier types, structured address, contacts) so the person-facing onboarding and the clinician verification queue both bind to one shape.
items.reshape: Source registration is a front-desk admin form (openhospital PatientInsert, openemr clerk screen). The enriched contract is the spine for patient-app self-edit (person surface) AND the registration-desk verify/merge/MRN-issue routes (management surface). Same contract, two surfaces.
items.no_loss_check: Preserve every source capability in the contract: PatientIdentifierType set (openmrs), person_patient_link person/contact split (openemr), AddressHierarchy levels (bahmni), merge/status. No PHI added to neutral core.patients shape; PHI stays in healthstack overlay (encrypted DOB/SSN).
items.license_status: openmrs MPL-2.0 reference-only; openemr/openhospital GPL-3.0 reference-only; bahmni AGPL-3.0 reference-only; vista Apache-2.0 safe-to-vendor. Mine ENTITY SHAPE only, no code copy from MPL/GPL/AGPL sources.
items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only: mine data-model shape into contract)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec
acceptance: patient-base-schema adds identifierType[] (typed: MRN/national-id/ssn-ref/other), structured address (country>region>district>locality hierarchy levels), contacts[] (person/related), and status enum
patient.tsp (neutral) carries no-PHI fields only; healthstack-patient.tsp carries PHI overlay fields (encrypted) referencing core via FK
SDK regenerates from the updated TypeSpec with zero manual edits to generated output
merge.ts handles the new fields (identifier/address/contact merge semantics)
items.test_plan: Unit: patient-base-schema.test.ts asserts new field shapes + enums; merge.test.ts asserts identifier/address/contact merge. Contract: SDK emit is byte-identical on re-run (idempotent). No-loss assertion mapping each source field (openmrs PatientIdentifierType, openemr person link, bahmni address level) to a contract field.
validation_commands: bun run --cwd curaos/tools/codegen build && bun curaos/tools/codegen/src/sdk-emit.ts patient-core-service
items.implementation_notes: patient-core is present-strong; this is an additive contract enrichment, not a rewrite. Address hierarchy auto-complete sources from geospatial-core-service (wire in a later slice). Keep neutral/PHI boundary: only references + metadata in core.patients.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Patient self-registration + demographics self-edit slice on patient-core-service (service after contract)
items.person_centric: The person registers themselves and owns subsequent edits; the clerk role is demoted to verify/merge/MRN-issue. The patient is the subject and owner of the demographics record.
items.reshape: Invert the source's clerk-first entry: patient-app POSTs a self-registration, landing in an unverified state; registration-desk management routes verify, assign identifier types, issue MRN, and resolve duplicates. Org process satisfied as a consequence of the person self-serving.
items.no_loss_check: Management surface keeps duplicate-merge, identifier-type assignment, MRN issuance, status transitions. Audit every demographic write (audit-core). PHI write path goes through healthstack-patient overlay only.
items.license_status: GPL/MPL reference-only; behavior re-implemented fresh from enriched contract, no source code copied.
items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) then native implementation against contract
items.generator_first_target: operation-slice (operation-slice-emit) on patient.tsp ops; service code consumes generated slice
acceptance: self-register endpoint creates patient in unverified status owned by the authenticated person (subject == requester)
self-edit endpoint allows the owning person to update own demographics; clinician role required for verify/merge/MRN-issue (roles.guard)
migration adds identifier-type/address-hierarchy/contact columns; drizzle schema matches contract; PHI columns live in healthstack overlay only
merge endpoint resolves two patient records preserving both identifier sets
items.test_plan: Unit: patients-service.test.ts + patients-dto.test.ts for self-register/self-edit/merge. Integration (real DB): patients-http.test.ts auth-matrix asserts person can self-edit own record, cannot edit others, clinician can verify/merge. Migration test asserts up/down. Audit-chain test asserts every write emits an audit event.
items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-1 contract. Reuse existing auth.guard/roles.guard/outbox already present in patient-core. Do not clobber existing patient-experience read-model.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Database-backed seed for enriched patient demographics (generator-first via service-seed-emit)
items.person_centric: Demo/dev/live-verify patients are real, person-owned records in the DB (identifiers, addresses, contacts), so both the patient portal and clinician search render truthful data, not API mocks.
items.reshape: Seed represents whole-person profiles (a person with their identifiers/address/contacts), not org rows, so the patient-app onboarding and chart show a coherent self.
items.no_loss_check: Seed exercises every enriched field (multi-identifier, address hierarchy, contacts, status) so no field is unverified. Replaces patient-app mock-data as the runtime data plane per demo-sample-data rule.
items.license_status: no source code reuse; synthetic non-PHI seed data only.
items.reuse_mode: C (generated scaffold: gen:service-seed)
items.generator_first_target: service-seed-emit (gen:service-seed) for patient-core-service
acceptance: service-seed-emit produces a patients.seed.ts covering enriched fields for >=10 synthetic persons across tenants
seed is idempotent (re-run does not duplicate) and writes through the real repository, not in-memory
no PHI in neutral seed; healthstack overlay seed carries encrypted DOB/SSN-ref for the same person IDs
items.test_plan: Run seed against a real ephemeral PG; assert row counts + idempotency on second run; assert patient-experience read-model returns seeded persons. Generator snapshot test in codegen __tests__ for the emitted seed shape.
items.implementation_notes: Generator-first: fold the enriched-field coverage into service-seed-emit so every service regen inherits it, not a hand-written seed. Removes need for patient-app/src/api/mock-data.ts at runtime.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Wire patient index into search-service + adopt clinician patient-search facets and saved cohorts/lists
items.person_centric: Search is a clinician-only management tool; the person never searches others (privacy preserved). The patient-app shows only the person's own record, never a search surface.
items.reshape: Keep search org-side: clinician patient-search panel + saved patient-lists/cohorts/flags. The searched patient sees nothing different. No person-facing search surface created.
items.no_loss_check: Preserve saved-list (openmrs Cohort), patient flags, recent-encounter ordering, and clinical-context filters (ward/clinic) as search facets. ehmp PatientSearchResult field set (icn, mostRecentEncounterDate) adopted.
items.license_status: ehmp/vista permissive (field set safe); openmrs MPL reference-only (Cohort concept only); openhospital GPL reference-only (UI pattern only).
items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) + adopt permissive ehmp field set
items.generator_first_target: na (search-service present-strong; wiring + facet config, no new generator)
acceptance: patient records (enriched) are indexed in search-service per-tenant index on create/update via outbox event
clinician search returns PatientSearchResult fields (name, MRN, icn-equivalent, mostRecentEncounterDate) ranked by recency
saved patient-lists/cohorts persist (Group/Cohort concept) and are clinician-scoped
no person-facing search route added to patient-app
items.test_plan: Integration: index a seeded patient, assert hybrid BM25+vector returns it with the field set; assert facet by ward/flag; assert saved-list create/read scoped to clinician role. Negative test: patient role cannot call search.
items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-2/3 (real patients to index). search-service already does cross-service hybrid search; this is wiring + facet config, not new infra.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: FamilyMemberHistory contract + SDK (FHIR R4), generator-first, before service
items.person_centric: Hereditary data is person-owned: the contract supports the patient building their own family tree; the clinician role confirms/curates. RelatedPerson reuses the personal-crm relationship graph to avoid re-keying relatives.
items.reshape: Source FamilyHistoryCPRS is a clinician chart tab. The contract supports a patient-app guided self-entry wizard (person surface) AND a clinician review/attest/CDS-trigger surface (management surface).
items.no_loss_check: Model relative + per-relative diseases/conditions + age-of-onset + vital-status (FHIR FamilyMemberHistory). Maps the CPRS 3-file model (FAMILY HISTORY / RELATIVE / RELATIVE DISEASES, files 500003/500015/500017) field-for-field. Nothing lost.
items.license_status: FamilyHistoryCPRS license UNKNOWN -> legal-review-required: DATA-MODEL SHAPE reference ONLY, no code reuse. openmrs-fhir2 MPL reference-only (RelatedPerson concept). maternity-tracker permissive.
items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only: data model only, NO code)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (FamilyMemberHistory.tsp) then sdk-emit, building on @curaos/fhir-client base
acceptance: FamilyMemberHistory TypeSpec covers patient ref, relationship (RelatedPerson), per-relative condition[] (coded), onset-age, deceased/vital-status, note
SDK emits from TypeSpec with no manual edits; fhir-client base types reused (not re-declared)
legal-review note recorded in spec header: data-model-only provenance, FamilyHistoryCPRS license unresolved
items.test_plan: Contract: TypeSpec compiles; SDK emit idempotent. No-loss mapping table in test/doc asserting each CPRS field (500003/500015/500017) maps to a FHIR FamilyMemberHistory element. fhir-client unit test for FamilyMemberHistory resource validation.
items.implementation_notes: healthstack-problems-service is scaffold-only (no src) - greenfield. Contract+SDK FIRST. Hereditary-risk CDS attaches in a later epic. Do NOT copy any FamilyHistoryCPRS code (license unknown).
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Family-history service slice on healthstack-problems-service (service after contract) + DB-backed seed
items.person_centric: The person owns and authors their family history; clinician attests. Self-entered relatives + conditions persist as real rows, surfaced to both the person (my-family-history) and clinician (review/attest).
items.reshape: Patient-authored entries land for clinician attestation rather than clinician-only entry. Org curation satisfied as a downstream step on the person's self-built tree.
items.no_loss_check: Relative, per-relative diseases/conditions, age-of-onset, vital-status, attestation status all persisted. Clinician attest + link-to-problems retained. Seed is DB-backed real data, not a mock.
items.license_status: data-model-only provenance (see XSRC-E1-5); fresh implementation, no code copied.
items.reuse_mode: E (port-adapt resource shape) + C (generated scaffold for service skeleton/seed)
items.generator_first_target: operation-slice-emit + service-seed-emit against the FamilyMemberHistory TypeSpec
acceptance: CRUD endpoints for FamilyMemberHistory keyed to a person; person can author/edit own, clinician can attest
migration + drizzle schema match the contract; relatives and per-relative conditions stored relationally
DB-backed seed creates family histories for seeded persons (links to XSRC-E1-3 patient IDs); idempotent
ConsentGuard applied so family-history reads honor patient consent
items.test_plan: Unit: family-history.service.test for author/attest state machine. Integration (real DB): create relative + conditions, read back via SDK, assert consent-gating denies an unconsented reader. Seed run + idempotency assertion.
items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-5 (contract) and XSRC-E1-9 (ConsentGuard wiring) for read-gating. Reuse RelatedPerson from personal-crm relationship graph where present to avoid re-keying relatives.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: ui-kit person-centric archetypes: Wizard/Stepper, Entity360/RecordChart, ConsentCard, FilterBar/SearchShell (generator-consumable)
items.person_centric: The journey archetypes (Wizard for self-registration + family-history, Entity360 for my-chart, ConsentCard for my-consents) make person-first surfaces possible instead of the source's generic CRUD admin forms.
items.reshape: Replaces the corpus's org-shaped single-CRUD-screen-per-service default with journey components the ui-app emitter can compose into person-facing flows.
items.no_loss_check: Management surfaces still get DataTable/forms (already present); these are additive person-journey archetypes. No existing ui-kit component removed.
source_file_refs: gap-analysis.json authored.functional_gaps (ui-kit archetype gap: Wizard/Stepper, RecordChart/Entity360, ConsentCard, FilterBar/SearchShell)
items.license_status: GPL/AGPL UI sources reference-only (pattern, not code). Components authored fresh.
items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) + F (fresh build into shared ui-kit)
items.generator_first_target: ui-kit shared components consumed by ui-app-emit/ui-app-files (NOT per-app bespoke)
acceptance: Wizard/Stepper, Entity360/RecordChart, ConsentCard, SearchShell exported from ui-kit index with tokens + a11y (keyboard nav, ARIA)
each has a unit/interaction test (shared-interactions pattern)
components are stateless/composable so ui-app-emit can wire them to SDK hooks
items.test_plan: Component tests: Wizard step nav + validation gate, Entity360 card render from data, ConsentCard grant/revoke callback, SearchShell debounce+filter. a11y assertions (roles, focus order).
items.implementation_notes: Generator-first surface: these are shared ui-kit primitives, NOT per-app screens. Next slice (E1-8) wires the ui-app emitter to consume them so every app inherits the archetypes. Use impeccable skill + OpenDesign for design per user directive.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Teach ui-app emitter person-journey surfaces (registration wizard, my-chart Entity360, my-consents, family-history wizard) - root-cause generator fix
items.person_centric: The emitter learns to produce person-first journey surfaces from service contracts, so patient-app (and future apps) get my-chart/my-consents/onboarding as the PRIMARY experience, not a generic org CRUD screen.
items.reshape: Per the gap (ui-app-emit infers ONE generic CRUD screen + single org-shaped surface), extend the emitter so a service flagged person-centric emits journey surfaces (Wizard/Entity360/ConsentCard) bound to its SDK, alongside the existing management surface.
items.no_loss_check: Management CRUD surface still emitted. Persona/journey emission is additive and contract-driven, so no app loses its admin capability. Bespoke screens in patient-app preserved (emit does not clobber hand-authored surfaces).
source_file_refs: gap-analysis.json authored.functional_gaps (ui-app-emit single generic CRUD screen gap)
items.license_status: no source code reuse; emitter logic authored fresh.
items.reuse_mode: generator-evolution (fold archetype emission into ui-app-emit/ui-app-files)
items.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit.ts + ui-app-files.ts (the emitter itself is the change)
acceptance: emitter, given a person-centric service contract, emits person-journey surface files (wizard/entity360/consent) wired to the generated SDK hooks
emitter still emits the management CRUD surface for the same service
re-running emit is idempotent and does NOT overwrite hand-authored bespoke surfaces (preserve-list honored)
snapshot test in codegen __tests__ covers the new emitted surface shapes
items.test_plan: Generator snapshot tests (codegen __tests__/templates + app-module-wire): assert journey + management surfaces emitted for a person-centric fixture service; assert bespoke-preserve; assert idempotent second emit produces no diff.
items.implementation_notes: ROOT-CAUSE fix per generator-evolution rule: the journey surfaces come from the emitter, never hand-edited per app. Depends on E1-7 (ui-kit archetypes exist) and E1-1 (contracts). This unblocks E1-10 (patient-app regen).
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: ConsentGuard wiring + patient-controlled my-consents on healthstack-consent-service (consent-gated reads across clinical services)
items.person_centric: Consent is person-controlled by design: the patient grants/revokes per-resource and per-recipient from patient-app; ConsentGuard enforces it on every clinical service read/write. Inverts the source's admin-set consent boolean.
items.reshape: OpenHospital's single PatientConsensus boolean DTO becomes granular person-owned consent. Person surface = my-consents (grant/revoke per data-category and recipient); compliance surface = consent audit + break-glass-with-reason + GDPR/HIPAA disclosure log.
items.no_loss_check: Granular per-resource+action consent EXCEEDS source. Break-glass (identity-service) + audit + disclosure log preserved. Local is stronger-than-source; no source code imported.
items.license_status: openhospital GPL-3.0 reference-only (concept not code); local consent stronger, nothing imported.
items.reuse_mode: H (reject inbound - local stronger) + wire existing @curaos/healthstack-consent ConsentGuard
items.generator_first_target: na (ConsentGuard pkg present; wire the guard + emit my-consents surface via E1-8 emitter)
acceptance: person can grant/revoke consent per data-category and per-recipient; persisted in healthstack-consent-service
ConsentGuard from @curaos/healthstack-consent gates family-history reads and patient-experience read-model reads by resource+action
break-glass access (identity-service) bypasses consent only with a logged reason captured to audit-core
consent changes emit an event; disclosure log queryable for compliance
items.test_plan: Unit: ConsentGuard allow/deny matrix (resource x action x recipient). Integration (real DB): grant then read succeeds, revoke then read denied (403), break-glass with reason succeeds + audit row written. Assert consent change emits AsyncAPI event.
items.implementation_notes: ConsentGuard pkg already exists (stronger-than-source). This is wiring the guard onto E1-2 (patient-experience) + E1-6 (family-history) read paths and exposing person-controlled grant/revoke. my-consents surface emitted by E1-8.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Patient portal tracer-bullet: regenerate patient-app from emitter (my-chart, my-consents, my-family-history, onboarding) on real BFF read-model
items.person_centric: The portal IS the primary person surface: the patient owns and reads their full chart, manages consents, builds family history, and self-registers. The org's record IS the patient's record presented to them.
items.reshape: Portal is the spine, not a bolt-on. One patient-experience read-model backs BOTH clinician chart and patient portal; no parallel PHR store as in OpenEMR.
items.no_loss_check: Attachments (storage/documents-core), flags, lists, secure messaging, AVS-placeholder all wired to existing services. Drifted action names (consent-core-service -> healthstack-consent-service; healthstack-care-plan-service -> healthstack-careplans-service) corrected. Runtime mock-data.ts removed in favor of seeded DB data.
items.license_status: openemr GPL + openmrs-distro AGPL reference-only (feature checklist, no code). maternity/pophealth permissive.
items.reuse_mode: C (generated app via ui-app-emit) on existing read-model + messaging contracts
items.generator_first_target: service-then-app: regenerate patient-app via the E1-8-extended emitter against patient-experience read-model + consent + family-history SDKs
acceptance: patient-app renders my-chart (Entity360 from patient-experience read-model), my-consents (ConsentCard grant/revoke), my-family-history (Wizard), self-registration onboarding (Wizard) using REAL seeded DB data via SDK, no runtime mocks
action filenames match canonical service names (consent-core-service -> healthstack-consent-service; healthstack-care-plan-service -> healthstack-careplans-service)
patient-app typechecks + builds clean; 401 redirects to auth; i18n switcher works
surfaces emitted by generator (E1-8), bespoke screens preserved
items.test_plan: Local JWT + Playwright sweep: log in as a seeded person, assert my-chart shows seeded allergies/problems/family-history, grant/revoke a consent and assert read-gating, complete onboarding wizard and assert a real patient row is created. Typecheck + build. Verify against running app (verify-before-build).
items.implementation_notes: TRACER BULLET that closes the epic: depends on E1-2/3 (real patients), E1-6 (family-history), E1-8 (emitter journey surfaces), E1-9 (consent). patient-app currently scaffold-only (surfaces/AppShell only) and has drifted action names + mock-data.ts. Fix the EMITTER (E1-8), do not hand-edit per-app. Remove mock-data.ts from the runtime data plane per demo-data rule.
items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

items.title: Make scheduling.tsp the real contract (appointments/providers/slots/patient-appointments/queue-lanes/desk-metrics) replacing the mold stub
items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
items.license_status: vista-m/RAPTOR Apache-2.0 safe-to-vendor-or-copy; openemr GPL-3.0 + openmrs-distro AGPL-3.0 = reference-only (no code lift). This item authors fresh TS contract from the EXISTING local controller routes; sources inform field-set only.
items.person_centric: person_surface = patient-app book/my-upcoming-visits/check-in/queue-position; management_surface = front-desk grid + provider templates + overbook + desk-metrics/alerts. Same Appointment/Slot/QueueEntry data, two re-centered SDK consumers.
items.no_loss_check: Queue/waiting-room lanes, provider+room scheduling, recurrence, find-next-slot, desk metrics/alerts all expressed as contract models; nothing the source desk grid does is dropped (room+find-next-slot land in XSRC-E2-7).
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec
acceptance: scheduling.tsp declares typed models + @route ops for EVERY route already in src/schedulings/schedulings.controller.ts: GET / (collection), GET desk-metrics, GET desk-alerts, GET appointments, GET providers, GET patients, GET bookable-providers, GET slots, GET patient-appointments, POST appointments (BookAppointmentInput), PATCH appointments/:id/lane (MoveLaneInput), POST patient-appointments (PatientBookInput), POST :id/advance, PATCH :id (ClinStatusInput); plus the generic admin GET /scheduling list+detail.

Model fields mirror the live Zod DTOs in scheduling.dto.ts (QueueLane enum: scheduled|checked-in|roomed|with-provider|checkout; ClinStatus enum: scheduled|checked-in|roomed|in-progress|complete|no-show) so SDK types == runtime validation.
bunx tsp compile specs/scheduling.tsp emits dist/openapi.yaml with all ops and zero diagnostics; the mold health/protected/whoami probes remain.
No em/en dashes; generator-provenance header preserved.
items.test_plan: spec:openapi compile gate (zero diagnostics); assert dist/openapi.yaml contains paths for /schedulings/appointments, /schedulings/slots, /schedulings/patient-appointments, /schedulings/appointments/{id}/lane, /scheduling (admin list). Contract-vs-controller route parity check (every controller @Get/@Post/@Patch has a tsp op).
items.implementation_notes: The controller + DTOs already encode the full surface; this item lifts that surface into the contract that was never updated past the mold. This is the generator-first prerequisite: no @curaos/scheduling-sdk can exist for these routes until the contract is real. Per [[curaos-generator-evolution-rule]], any model shape that should apply to EVERY service goes in tools/codegen/templates/service-core/specs/, but appointment/slot/queue models are domain-specific so they live in this service tsp.
items._epic: Clinic scheduling (appointment/provider/room scheduling, queue, triage)

items.title: Generate @curaos/scheduling-sdk from the real scheduling OpenAPI
items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
items.license_status: First-party generated client; no external code. Mirrors @curaos/auth-sdk generation from identity-service/specs/auth.tsp.
items.person_centric: One typed client both the patient-app (self-book/queue) and front-office/clinician (desk/board) consume; the dual surfaces share the same SDK methods.
items.no_loss_check: Every contract op from XSRC-E2-1 yields an SDK method; nothing unreachable.
items.generator_first_target: sdk-package
acceptance: Codegen sdk subcommand consumes scheduling-service/dist/openapi.yaml and emits @curaos/scheduling-sdk with typed methods for bookAppointment, listSlots, listBookableProviders, patientBook, moveLane, advance, setClinStatus, listAppointments, listPatientAppointments, deskMetrics, deskAlerts.
SDK typechecks and exports request/response types derived from the contract models (QueueLane, ClinStatus enums included).
sdk-emit test asserts the scheduling client is generated identically to the auth-sdk shape (no bespoke hand-edits).
items.test_plan: Run sdk-emit codegen test; typecheck the emitted package; assert generated method names match contract operationIds. NO manual edits to generated output (generator-first; any edge case folds back into sdk-emit).
items.implementation_notes: Pure generator step: feed the now-real OpenAPI through the existing @hey-api/openapi-ts lane the platform already uses. If the emitter can't express a needed method shape, fix the emitter (curaos/tools/codegen), not the generated package.
items._epic: Clinic scheduling (appointment/provider/room scheduling, queue, triage)

items.title: Add real Appointment/Slot/ProviderSchedule/QueueEntry Drizzle tables + forward migration to scheduling-service
items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
items.license_status: Field set informed by vista-m File 44 APPOINTMENT (Apache-2.0, copyable pattern) + openmrs QueueEntry/QueueRoom (AGPL reference-only). Schema authored fresh; no GPL/AGPL code lifted.
items.person_centric: Tables are the neutral spine both surfaces read. Slot = published bookable window (drives patient self-book); QueueEntry.lane drives the person's queue-position view AND the desk board.
items.no_loss_check: Appointment status lifecycle (scheduled..no-show), provider+slot linkage, queue lane, double-booking-prevention unique index, recurrence reference to calendar-core all present; replaces the in-memory front-desk.read-model.ts seed with no field loss (the seed shape is the column source-of-truth).
items.generator_first_target: service
acceptance: schema.ts adds tables in scheduling_core: appointment (id, tenant_id, patient_ref, provider_ref, slot_id, calendar_event_uid, start_at timestamptz, duration_min, reason, status, lane, created/updated), slot (id, tenant_id, provider_ref, start_at, duration_min, published bool, capacity), provider_schedule (id, tenant_id, provider_ref, template/recurrence ref to calendar-core), queue_entry (id, tenant_id, appointment_id, lane, priority, entered_at).
All timestamps are timestamptz; tenant_id present on every table (Citus shard-key per [[curaos-postgres-rule]]); no PHI columns (patient/provider are references, per AGENTS.md PHI boundary).
Unique index prevents double-booking a provider+slot; lane CHECK constraint matches QueueLaneSchema.
Forward-only migration generated via drizzle-kit; schema.ts == SQL == meta snapshot (the 3-artifact invariant); per [[curaos-rolling-update-rule]] no -v2 parallel path.
Seed migration (XSRC-E2-3 ships skeleton; XSRC-E2-4 fills) keyed to DEMO_TENANT_ID a1f0a1f0-...001 with ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING.
items.test_plan: drizzle-kit generate produces no drift; migration applies clean against a scratch DB; assert double-booking unique index rejects a duplicate provider+slot insert (one runnable check); assert lane CHECK rejects an invalid lane.
items.implementation_notes: Column list MUST be derived from front-desk.read-model.ts + scheduling-admin.read-model.ts so the swap from in-memory seed to repo is field-for-field (the read-model header documents this exact swap-point). Use bun run gen:service-seed to emit the seed skeleton (correct next migration number + journal entry) rather than hand-deriving it.
items._epic: Clinic scheduling (appointment/provider/room scheduling, queue, triage)

items.title: Repo-back the scheduling service: replace in-memory front-desk read-model with Drizzle repo + real demo seed
items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
items.license_status: First-party. No external code.
items.person_centric: Patient self-book (POST patient-appointments) and queue-position read resolve against real rows; front-desk board + desk-metrics read the same rows. Both surfaces now show identical persisted truth.
items.no_loss_check: Every method the in-memory read-models expose (adminList, appointments, providers, slots, patientAppointments, deskMetrics, deskAlerts, board lanes) is reimplemented against the repo with the same envelope shapes; no route or RBAC change; no capability dropped.
items.generator_first_target: service

items.title: Closed-loop critical-result follow-up: event-driven escalation across orders + tasks-core + notify (+ patient next-step)
items.person_centric: Source (AWARE) is a clinician acknowledgement + QI-supervisor system; the person is invisible. Re-center so the person is never the last to know: when a critical result needs action the patient-app delivers a clear next-step instruction (call your doctor / come in) while the clinician escalation chain runs in parallel. Management surface: critical-result inbox, acknowledgement, no-ack escalation chain, who-saw-when audit.
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (AWARE VEFA.AlertCache + escalation-timestamp + follow-up-action model); pattern over existing notify+tasks primitives
items.license_status: aware permissive (Apache-2.0 family) = safe-to-vendor port-adapt.

no-ack timer escalates up a configurable chain with escalation timestamps recorded
acknowledgement closes the loop and is audited (who-saw-when)
patient receives a plain-language next-step notification via their chosen channel
event payloads carry references only (no PHI), per the data-boundary rule

items.no_loss_check: Acknowledgement tracking, escalation timestamps/chain, follow-up action prompts, audit all preserved; patient added to the loop without removing clinician escalation.

items.person_centric: Cadence is invisible infra; it powers the person-facing 'my subscription / my agreement / my recurring invoice / my recurring gift' self-manage surfaces (next-due date, pause, cancel-at-period-end) instead of org-only billing schedulers.
items.no_loss_check: All cadence semantics already proven in donation-core (frequency, next_due_at, BullMQ trigger, month-end clamp) preserved verbatim; nothing dropped, only generalized so subscription/contract/recurring-invoice reuse one primitive (DRY).

Runnable assert/demo check covering month-end clamp (Jan 31 -> Feb 28/29) and each frequency
items.test_plan: Unit: month-end clamp vectors (31st across short months, leap year), each frequency interval; assert nextDueAt is deterministic. Reuse donation-core's existing recurrence tests retargeted at the shared helper.

Extract the smallest pure function donation already proved, plus a codegen partial so subscription/contract/recurring-invoice services wire the same BullMQ trigger pattern. currency converts amounts using a rate-as-of, returns int64 cents, and computes an FX gain/loss delta on settlement vs original booking rate

No verbatim Odoo source lifted - implementation is original TS (license gate)
items.test_plan: Unit money paths with explicit vectors: compound tax order, repartition split sums to gross, withholding portion, FX settlement gain and loss both produce a balancing delta. These are money-path checks (non-negotiable per ponytail).

items.implementation_notes: These two packages are blocking dependencies for AR invoicing, multi-currency GL, and expense posting. Build them standalone with vectors before any service wiring. Ponytail: do not scaffold per-jurisdiction localization now (gap explicitly defers localization as YAGNI / codegen-emitter follow-up).

items.license_status: first-party (CuraOS) safe-to-vendor
source_file_refs: .ai-analysis/ui-visual-inventory.json#generator_targets[contract lint so a screen archetype that needs a search endpoint fails generation]
items.reuse_mode: first-party generator evolution
items.person_centric: Prevents emitting a beautiful person-facing search-shell or record-chart over a route that 404s, which is the data-truth failure the prior wave hit. Keeps person surfaces honest: no archetype renders unless its data plane exists.
items.no_loss_check: Lint only blocks generation of an archetype whose backing contract operation is missing; it removes nothing, it forces the contract/route to exist first (generator-first ordering).
items.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit archetype-binding lint reading @curaos/contracts declared list/detail/search/action operations
acceptance: An archetype that requires search (search-shell, faceted list) fails generation with a clear error if the service contract declares no search operation
record-chart fails if detail + related-list operations are absent; ledger fails without line-item ops
Error names the missing contract operation + the service so the fix is to add the contract op (not hand-edit the app)
Existing apps with valid contracts still generate clean
items.test_plan: ui-app-emit.test.ts: fixture contract missing search -> generating a search-shell throws with the operation name; fixture with search -> generates; record-chart without detail op throws.
items.implementation_notes: Depends on E10-9 archetype vocabulary. ponytail: read the operations already known to the SDK/contract layer (operation-slice-emit already enumerates ops), do not parse .tsp again. Fail-closed, clear message pointing at the contract owner.

items.title: Generator: ui-app-native-emit real data-bound patient-app + clinician-app screens (not placeholder tabs)
items.license_status: first-party generator; ehmp MIT pattern safe-to-vendor; openemr/openmrs reference-only
items.reuse_mode: first-party generator evolution + E port-adapt (ehmp screen model)
items.person_centric: patient-app/clinician-app are scaffold-only today. Generator must emit real data-bound mobile screens (appointment, messaging, billing, consent, care-plan, vitals, lab-results, problems, meds) from the SAME SDK hooks the web generator uses, so the person carries their journey on mobile, not placeholder tabs.
items.no_loss_check: clinician-app retains full clinical screens (mgmt); patient-app re-centers same data as person journey. No web screen capability is mobile-dropped; mobile maps to the same SDK ops.
items.generator_first_target: ui-app-native-emit screen emission bound to @curaos/*-sdk hooks (parity with ui-app-emit archetypes)
acceptance: Emitted patient-app screens (appointment/messaging/billing/consent/care-plan/vitals/lab-results/problems/meds) bind to real SDK hooks, not placeholder content
clinician-app emits the management variant of the same screens
Screens render seeded data out of the box (data-truth) and typecheck + bundle
Shares entity schema + SDK hooks with web ui-app-emit (one contract, two platforms)
items.test_plan: ui-app-native-emit test: emit patient-app fixture -> assert SDK hook imports + screen list (not placeholder); bundle/typecheck the emitted Expo app in CI harness; assert seeded content renders.
validation_commands: cd curaos/tools/codegen && bun run src/index.ts ui-app-native patient-app --dry-run
items.implementation_notes: Depends on E10-9 archetype/surface model + E10-10 contract lint (mobile screens need the same real endpoints). ponytail: reuse the web archetype-to-SDK-hook mapping; do not author a second mapping for native. RN component equivalents only where Radix/web ui-kit cannot run.

items.title: Regenerate the 14 scaffold apps + dual-surface front-office/patient/clinician with the new archetypes (tracer-bullet: front-office + patient-app first)
items.license_status: first-party (CuraOS) safe-to-vendor
source_file_refs: .ai-analysis/ui-visual-inventory.json#screens (P0/P1 screens per app: registration, chart-360, scheduling, billing, consent, crm-pipeline)
items.reuse_mode: first-party generator output (no hand-edits; archetype hints in app docs only)
items.person_centric: Prove the dual-surface generator end-to-end on the highest-value vertical slice: front-office (management) + patient-app (person) over the SAME patient/billing/consent contracts. Person sees registration wizard + my-health record-chart + my-appointments + pay-my-bill + consent; management sees the full admin variants.
items.no_loss_check: Management front-office keeps every existing screen + full archetype controls; patient-app person surface is additive. Regeneration must not drop bespoke screens already shipped in the 10 more-complete apps (preserve bespoke namespaces).
items.generator_first_target: regeneration via ui-app-emit/ui-app-native-emit only; any defect folds back to the generator (E10-9/10/11), never a per-app patch
acceptance: front-office regenerated with management surface: patient-search (faceted), patient-chart record-chart, scheduling board, billing ledger, consent
patient-app regenerated with person surface: registration wizard, my-health record-chart (collapsed), my-appointments calendar, pay-my-bill, consent card
Both bind to real (data-truth) endpoints; person never sees clinician jargon/8-tabs by default
Any edge case hit during regen is fixed in the generator + re-emitted (zero special per-app edits), proof documented
Apps typecheck + build green
items.test_plan: Regenerate front-office + patient-app; typecheck + build; Playwright local-JWT sweep that every emitted screen renders real seeded data (per curaos full-surface-sweep rule). If a screen 404s, root-cause in generator or contract (E10-10 lint), not the app.
validation_commands: cd curaos/tools/codegen && bun run src/index.ts ui-app front-office --write
cd curaos/tools/codegen && bun run src/index.ts ui-app-native patient-app --write
cd curaos/frontend/apps/front-office && bun run typecheck && bun run build
items.implementation_notes: BLOCKED by in-flight generator barrier until E10-9/10/11 land (regenerating now bakes in the defect being removed). Tracer-bullet: front-office + patient-app ONE vertical slice first, then fan out to the other 12 scaffold apps. ponytail: preserve bespoke screens via the existing bespoke-namespace mechanism; do not clobber the 10 more-complete apps' hand-built screens.

items.title: Builder surfaces: dashboard/workspace surface type + per-user SavedLayout (contract-first) for drag-drop self-serve dashboards
items.license_status: safe-to-vendor (ehmp-app MIT, copy-verbatim screen/workspace model w/ attribution); suitecrm AGPL = reference-only
source_file_refs: .ai-analysis/source-to-local-map.json (platform-cross: User-defined dashboard/screen layout -> builder-core-service, partial, port-adapt, gft=contract-typespec)
items.reuse_mode: A copy-verbatim (ehmp screen/workspace model, MIT w/ NOTICE) + E port-adapt
items.person_centric: Person surface = render MY saved dashboard layout (my-overview home). Management surface = builder-studio drag-drop dashboard authoring + per-user SavedLayout (tenant_id, user_id). One builder service, two surfaces.
items.no_loss_check: Full drag-drop widget/applet workspace authoring + per-user saved layouts preserved (ehmp UserDefinedScreens parity); person gets the simple render path.
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec: DashboardSurface + SavedLayout in builder-core specs, regenerate builder-sdk, THEN service CRUD + builder-studio surface (generator-first landing order)
acceptance: DashboardSurface + per-user SavedLayout (tenant_id, user_id) defined in builder.tsp contract; builder-sdk regenerated
builder-core CRUD persists layouts in DB (data-truth, not in-memory) with gen:service-seed sample layouts
builder-studio drag-drop authoring saves a layout; person app renders the saved layout (read path)
RBAC: person-scoped (own) vs admin-scoped layouts
items.test_plan: Contract regen lint green; builder-core integration test saves + loads a layout from DB (real repo, not mock); builder-studio renders authored layout; person render path loads saved layout. Seed produces a sample layout.
items.implementation_notes: Generator-first: contract -> sdk -> service -> builder-studio surface. Reuses the existing builder-core surface mechanism (present-strong) + adds dashboard surface type. ponytail: SavedLayout is a JSON column keyed by (tenant,user,surface), not a new normalized widget schema. ehmp model is the only verbatim copy (NOTICE).

items.title: Reports builder surface: ReportDefinition contract + field catalog from @curaos/contracts (closes generator field-catalog gap) feeding ReportViewer
items.license_status: frappe MIT = safe-to-vendor (port report-definition schema); ehmp MIT = safe-to-vendor; suitecrm AGPL + odoo LGPL = reference-only
source_file_refs: .ai-analysis/source-to-local-map.json (platform-cross: Self-serve report/query builder -> reports-service, present-weak, port-adapt, gft=contract-typespec)
target_files: curaos/tools/codegen/src (field catalog from contract entity definitions)
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (frappe report-definition schema, MIT) + first-party generator closure (field catalog from contracts)
items.person_centric: Person surface = my-overview KPI home (simple). Management surface = define own reports as first-party saved objects (fields/group-by/filters/chart/scheduled delivery) run through existing Gotenberg/Superset. ReportViewer (E10-8) is the shared render surface.
items.no_loss_check: Field select/group/filter/chart/scheduled delivery all preserved (frappe query-report parity); person gets simple my-overview + adaptive forms. reports-service Superset embed + PDF kept.
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec ReportDefinition + field-catalog-from-contracts generator closure (builder lists real typed fields from @curaos/contracts entity definitions), regenerate reports-sdk, THEN service CRUD+run, THEN ReportViewer binding
acceptance: ReportDefinition (source-entity ref, fields from contract catalog, group-by, filter, chart, schedule) defined in contract; reports-sdk regenerated
Field catalog is driven from @curaos/contracts entity definitions (generator closure), not hand-listed; a lint proves the catalog matches the contract
reports-service CRUD + run feeds Gotenberg/Superset; gen:service-seed sample report definitions (data-truth)
person-scoped + admin-scoped via RBAC; ReportViewer (E10-8) runs a definition
items.test_plan: Contract regen + field-catalog-from-contract lint green; reports-service integration test creates + runs a definition against real data; seed produces a sample report; ReportViewer renders run output. ponytail self-check: catalog field count equals contract entity field count.
items.implementation_notes: Risk = scope creep into full BI; Superset already covers dashboards, so STOP at report-definition + run + export, do not build a query engine. Generator-first: the field-catalog-from-contracts closure is the reusable win (one item later reuses it for the builder field picker). frappe schema is the port source (MIT, NOTICE).

items.title: Contract-first ReportDefinition entity in reports.tsp (dataset/fields/group-by/filter/chart/schedule) + regenerate reports-sdk
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (Frappe report.py + SuiteCRM AOR shape, schema only)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec
items.person_centric: Same ReportDefinition contract drives a person-scoped (my-data) report and an admin-scoped (full) report via RBAC scope; person-facing reports name fields in plain terms, no FHIR/SQL syntax exposed.
items.no_loss_check: Field select, group-by, filter conditions, chart types, and scheduled delivery (already present in reports-service via scheduled jobs) are all carried into the typed contract; no SuiteCRM AOR (AOR_Report/AOR_Charts/AOR_Scheduled_Reports) capability dropped, definition is now interoperable via @curaos/contracts rather than PHP.
items.license_status: Frappe MIT (safe-to-vendor-or-copy, schema port-adapt) + pophealth Apache-2.0 (cohort input); SuiteCRM AGPL-3.0 reference-only - shape inspiration ONLY, no code copied.
items.reuse_mode_detail: port-adapt the report-definition data model; do not vendor PHP.
items.description: Replace the placeholder ReportsRead/ReportsWriteInput models in reports.tsp with real ReportDefinition entities: dataset/source-entity ref, selected fields, group-by, filter conditions, chart spec, schedule (reuse existing scheduled-jobs). Regenerate reports-sdk from the updated TypeSpec (zero hand-written transport). This is the generator-first prerequisite for E11-2; the entity must enter via the contract before any service code.
items.implementation_notes: reports.tsp currently only has generic HealthStatus/ReportsRead/ReportsWriteInput scaffolding (namespace CuraOS.Reports). Model the field list as a reference into the contract entity catalog so the builder can list real typed fields automatically. Chart spec stays a small typed union (bar/line/pie/table/number) - no charting lib in the contract.
acceptance: reports.tsp defines ReportDefinition with fields[], groupBy[], filters[], chart, schedule, scope (person|tenant), and CRUD + run ops
reports-sdk regenerates with no hand-edited transport files
field references resolve against @curaos/contracts entity catalog (typed, not free strings)
no SuiteCRM PHP or AGPL code copied; only Frappe/pophealth permissive shapes referenced
items.test_plan: Codegen snapshot test for reports-sdk generation; TypeSpec compile check; assert ReportDefinition emits the 6 capability groups (fields/group-by/filter/chart/schedule/scope). Golden snapshot in curaos/tools/codegen/__tests__.
validation_commands: cd curaos/backend/services/reports-service && bunx tsp compile specs

items.title: ReportDefinition CRUD + run endpoint in reports-service against the field catalog (tracer-bullet: define -> run -> render)
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (execution semantics from Frappe query_report; output via existing Gotenberg/Superset)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (consumed; service implements generated SDK)
items.person_centric: Person can save and run their own ad-hoc report (pick fields, group, filter, chart) as a first-party saved object; admin gets the same engine with full scope. The org-centric AOR builder is re-centered so my-data reports are the default path for the person.
items.no_loss_check: Render + schedule + PDF (Gotenberg) + Superset embed already in reports-service are preserved; the only addition is the first-party definition engine the source had and local lacked. No existing reports-service capability removed.
items.license_status: Frappe MIT execution semantics port-adapt; output stack (Gotenberg, Superset) already Apache-2.0 in-tree.
items.description: Implement ReportDefinition CRUD + run endpoint in reports-service/src/reports executing against the contract field catalog and feeding Gotenberg (PDF) / Superset (embed) for output. Drive the field catalog from contract entity definitions so the builder lists real typed fields automatically (closes the generator gap). Add migration for report_definition table keyed (tenant_id, owner_user_id, scope).
items.implementation_notes: Depends on E11-1 SDK. Run path: resolve fields -> build safe parameterized query against the referenced dataset -> shape rows -> hand to existing PDF/embed output. Person scope filters rows to owner; tenant scope is RBAC-gated. ponytail: reuse existing scheduled-jobs and Gotenberg providers in src/providers, do not add a new scheduler.
acceptance: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /report-definitions and POST /report-definitions/{id}/run work against a real DB-backed table
run executes group-by + filters and returns typed rows; chart spec passed through to output
person scope returns only the owner's rows; tenant scope honors RBAC
PDF output routes through existing Gotenberg provider (no new dependency)
items.test_plan: Integration test (real Postgres, not mocked): seed two tenants + a person, define a report, run it, assert group-by/filter correctness and scope isolation. Contract test reports-sdk <-> service. E2E: define -> run -> PDF bytes returned.
validation_commands: cd curaos/backend/services/reports-service && bun run db:migrate && bun run test:integration

items.title: Author healthstack-quality.tsp contract: Measure, PopulationCriteria (10 codes), MeasureResult, Cohort, Baseline + generate @curaos/healthstack-quality-sdk
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (pophealth/HDS HQMF model -> TypeSpec entities)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec
items.person_centric: Same Measure/PopulationCriteria contract yields a patient-facing 'my care gaps & screenings due' surface AND a management population/practice dashboard; the contract carries both scopes, person surface is the default for patient-app.
items.no_loss_check: All 10 ALL_POPULATION_CODES (IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/DENEXCEP/NUMEX/MSRPOPL/OBSERV/STRAT and population set), stratification, continuous-variable (OBSERV) measures, baselines/targets, and cohort filtering are explicitly modeled as contract entities; nothing from pophealth measure.rb / HDS population_criteria.rb dropped.
items.license_status: pophealth + health-data-standards Apache-2.0 (safe-to-vendor-or-copy with NOTICE); TS port is clean-room from the documented model.
items.description: healthstack-quality-service is scaffold-only (README + chart only, 'HEDIS/CMS eCQM TBD'). Generator-first: author the quality contract (Measure, PopulationCriteria with the 10 codes, MeasureResult, Cohort, Baseline/Target, MeasureStratum entities + ops) and regenerate the service skeleton + new @curaos/healthstack-quality-sdk. Entity must enter via .tsp, not hand-written service code. Any edge case (e.g. CV/OBSERV measure shape) folds back into the service-core spec template per generator-evolution rule.
items.implementation_notes: Population codes are an enum carried verbatim from HDS. Baseline/target are contract-configured numeric thresholds. Scope field (person|practice) selects the surface. This is the keystone for E11-5/6/7.
acceptance: healthstack-quality.tsp models Measure, PopulationCriteria (all 10 codes as enum), MeasureResult, Cohort, Baseline, Stratum
service skeleton + @curaos/healthstack-quality-sdk generate cleanly with no hand-written transport
OBSERV/continuous-variable and STRAT shapes representable in the contract
Apache-2.0 NOTICE recorded for HDS-derived model
items.test_plan: TypeSpec compile; codegen snapshot for the new sdk; assert all 10 population codes present in generated enum. Snapshot in curaos/tools/codegen/__tests__.

items.title: Port population-criteria eval + measure-calc into healthstack-quality-service/src/measures (TS) with golden-file tests vs pophealth/HDS
items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (TS measure-eval engine, golden-file vs Ruby reference)
items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (consumed)
items.person_centric: The single measure-eval engine produces both the population/practice dashboard (management) and the per-patient care-gap nudge (person) - one engine, two surfaces, instead of a separate clinical-decision-support rebuild.
items.no_loss_check: All 10 population codes, stratification, OBSERV continuous-variable measures, and cohort filtering produce outputs matching pophealth/HDS golden files; if any source measure semantic is not yet reproducible it is filed forward, not silently dropped.
items.license_status: pophealth + HDS Apache-2.0; Ruby retained as golden-file reference (run for test oracle), TS engine is the shipping path.
items.description: Tracer-bullet core: implement the eCQM execution engine in TS (population-criteria evaluation + measure calculation over a patient cohort) driven by the E11-4 contract. Add golden-file tests comparing TS engine output to pophealth/HDS reference outputs for representative proportion + CV measures.
items.implementation_notes: Depends on E11-4 SDK and E11-6 cohort source. Start with one proportion measure end to end (cohort -> IPP/DENOM/NUMER -> result) as the tracer, then add DENEX/DENEXCEP/NUMEX, STRAT, OBSERV. Keep the Ruby reference invocable in CI as the oracle. ponytail: do not abstract a plugin system for measure types until >1 calc family proven.
acceptance: engine computes IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/DENEXCEP/NUMEX for at least one real proportion measure
STRAT and OBSERV (continuous-variable) measures supported
golden-file tests: TS output matches pophealth/HDS reference within defined tolerance
baselines/targets read from contract config
items.test_plan: Golden-file suite: fixed patient bundles -> TS engine vs Ruby HDS oracle, assert population counts + CV observations equal. Unit tests per population code. Integration: run a measure over a seeded cohort.

items.title: Cohort/population-filtering source + value-set binding via terminology-service $expand (api-adapter)
items.reuse_mode: D api-adapter (terminology-service $expand) + E port-adapt (cohort model from pophealth)
items.generator_first_target: asyncapi-event (ValueSetUpdated subscription)
items.person_centric: Accurate value-set membership makes the person's 'care gaps' matching correct (e.g. the right screening codes); management sees which value sets a measure binds and can re-expand on terminology update.
items.no_loss_check: OID-based value-set references, expansion-driven membership, update-invalidation, and pophealth cohort/patient-filtering semantics all preserved; terminology-service is already real-working (Snowstorm $expand) so no terminology capability rebuilt or lost.
items.license_status: pophealth Apache-2.0 cohort model; terminology-service already in-tree.
items.description: Implement cohort/patient-filtering (population cohort definition) feeding the E11-5 engine, and bind measure codes to value sets by calling terminology-service $expand (api-adapter) for member codes. Subscribe to terminology ValueSetUpdated to invalidate cached expansions.
items.implementation_notes: Reuse terminology-sdk $expand; do not re-implement terminology. Cache expansions keyed by value-set OID + version; invalidate on ValueSetUpdated event. ponytail: cache invalidation via the existing event bus, no new infra.
acceptance: cohort definition selects a patient population by filter criteria
measure code -> value set -> member codes resolved via terminology $expand
ValueSetUpdated event invalidates the cached expansion and triggers re-eval
binding visible to management surface (which value sets a measure uses)
items.test_plan: Integration with real terminology-service: bind a measure to a known value set, assert membership codes; emit ValueSetUpdated, assert cache invalidated. Cohort filter unit tests.

items.title: HQMF measure import + QRDA I/III generation as Apache-2.0 background converter behind conversion-core-service
items.reuse_mode: C run-as-background-service (wrap HDS Ruby with NOTICE) + A copy-verbatim
items.generator_first_target: asyncapi-event (MeasureImported)
items.person_centric: Indirect for HQMF (correct measures -> accurate 'my care gaps'); QRDA I export powers patient-app 'export my record' so the person can take a QRDA I / C-CDA to another provider. Management gets QRDA III for CMS/payer submission.
items.no_loss_check: HQMF document model, data_criteria, population_criteria, precondition trees, round-trip generator, and QRDA Cat I + Cat III + C32 + CDA validation are all preserved via the wrapped HDS lib; nothing reimplemented-and-lost.
items.license_status: health-data-standards Apache-2.0 (copy-verbatim-ok-with-NOTICE) - safe to run the Ruby HDS lib as-is behind a service boundary with NOTICE file.
items.description: Wrap the HDS hqmf-parser as an Apache-2.0 background converter behind conversion-core-service (already has PDF/FHIR/HL7/CSV + async job queue): import HQMF/eCQM definitions -> emit MeasureImported event that healthstack-quality-service subscribes to. Same boundary generates QRDA Cat I (per-patient) + Cat III (aggregate), routed through clinical-doc-service CDA bridge for C-CDA validation.
items.implementation_notes: Run-as-background-service (do NOT port HQMF/QRDA XML into TS). conversion-core-service already has the async job queue - reuse it. clinical-doc-service already real-working (CDA bridge) - reuse for validation. Add NOTICE per Apache-2.0. ponytail: wrap, don't rewrite the standards parser.
acceptance: uploaded HQMF definition is parsed by the wrapped HDS lib and emits MeasureImported
healthstack-quality-service consumes MeasureImported and persists the executable measure
QRDA Cat I and Cat III generated; C-CDA validated via clinical-doc-service
NOTICE file present for the vendored Apache-2.0 lib
items.test_plan: Integration: import a real eCQM HQMF, assert MeasureImported event + persisted measure; generate QRDA III for a seeded cohort and validate against HDS validator (golden). E2E: patient 'export my record' returns valid QRDA I.
validation_commands: cd curaos/backend/services/conversion-core-service && bun run build && bun run test:integration

acceptance: @curaos/policy is no longer clean-slate: policy.tsp defines Role, Permission(resource+action+scope), FieldPermission(field, role, read|write|mask), AbacRule(subject/resource/environment attribute conditions), AccessDecision(allow|deny+reason)

This is the generator-first root for items E13-2/3/4. Do NOT add the engine here yet (E13-2); this item is the contract only.

items.title: Extend consent enforcement from healthstack-only to neutral PII boundary via mold ConsentGuard gating
items.reuse_mode: H reject inbound (local healthstack-consent stronger) + G pattern-reference-only (openhospital PatientConsensus shape)
items.license_status: openhospital-api=GPL-3.0 reference-only (consent endpoint shape only, no code); local @curaos/healthstack-consent first-party
items.generator_first_target: codegen mold (controller.hbs ConsentGuard wiring) - currently gated behind {{#if healthstackOverlay}} only; extend to neutral PII-flagged resources
items.person_centric: Consent enforcement is the person's data-flow toggle (deny -> 403 read AND write). Today only healthstack overlay services honor it; the person's PII in neutral services (party, crm, documents) is NOT consent-gated. Extends the person's control over ALL their data, not just clinical.
items.no_loss_check: Existing healthstack ConsentGuard + RequiresConsent (consent-evaluator/observer/enforcer, mold-wired, fail-closed) fully preserved. Neutral gating is opt-in via a PII flag on the resource contract so non-PII services keep current behavior. openhospital consent CRUD capability (per-patient consent record) already exceeded by local evaluator.
acceptance: controller.hbs ConsentGuard is applicable to neutral services carrying a PII-resource flag (not only healthstackOverlay), keeping fail-closed semantics
consent-enforcement.test.ts.hbs asserts neutral PII resource denies read+write on consent reject (status 403) and allows on accept
non-PII neutral services regenerate with NO consent guard (unchanged)
@curaos/healthstack-consent enforcer reused as-is (no fork); only the mold wiring condition broadens
items.test_plan: Generator: render controller for a PII-flagged neutral service, assert ConsentGuard in @UseGuards; render non-PII, assert absent. Integration (mold template test): consent reject -> 403 for read AND write; accept -> 200. Reuse existing consent-evaluator.test.ts.
items.implementation_notes: Verified: controller.hbs wires ConsentGuard only under {{#if healthstackOverlay}}; @curaos/healthstack-consent is the mature shared owner. Generator-first: broaden the mold condition + the emitted consent-enforcement test, do not hand-wire per service. Confirm with data-boundary rule that PII flag drives the gate (PHI/PII -> overlay/consent-gated).
items.risk: Over-gating could 403 legitimate neutral reads; mitigate by requiring an explicit PII flag on the contract resource (default off).
validation_commands: cd curaos/tools/codegen && bun run typecheck && bun test

flat_items.title: Patient self-registration + demographics self-edit slice on patient-core-service (service after contract)
flat_items.person_centric: The person registers themselves and owns subsequent edits; the clerk role is demoted to verify/merge/MRN-issue. The patient is the subject and owner of the demographics record.
flat_items.reshape: Invert the source's clerk-first entry: patient-app POSTs a self-registration, landing in an unverified state; registration-desk management routes verify, assign identifier types, issue MRN, and resolve duplicates. Org process satisfied as a consequence of the person self-serving.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Management surface keeps duplicate-merge, identifier-type assignment, MRN issuance, status transitions. Audit every demographic write (audit-core). PHI write path goes through healthstack-patient overlay only.
flat_items.license_status: GPL/MPL reference-only; behavior re-implemented fresh from enriched contract, no source code copied.
flat_items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) then native implementation against contract
flat_items.generator_first_target: operation-slice (operation-slice-emit) on patient.tsp ops; service code consumes generated slice
acceptance: self-register endpoint creates patient in unverified status owned by the authenticated person (subject == requester)
self-edit endpoint allows the owning person to update own demographics; clinician role required for verify/merge/MRN-issue (roles.guard)
migration adds identifier-type/address-hierarchy/contact columns; drizzle schema matches contract; PHI columns live in healthstack overlay only
merge endpoint resolves two patient records preserving both identifier sets
flat_items.test_plan: Unit: patients-service.test.ts + patients-dto.test.ts for self-register/self-edit/merge. Integration (real DB): patients-http.test.ts auth-matrix asserts person can self-edit own record, cannot edit others, clinician can verify/merge. Migration test asserts up/down. Audit-chain test asserts every write emits an audit event.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-1 contract. Reuse existing auth.guard/roles.guard/outbox already present in patient-core. Do not clobber existing patient-experience read-model.
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: Database-backed seed for enriched patient demographics (generator-first via service-seed-emit)
flat_items.person_centric: Demo/dev/live-verify patients are real, person-owned records in the DB (identifiers, addresses, contacts), so both the patient portal and clinician search render truthful data, not API mocks.
flat_items.reshape: Seed represents whole-person profiles (a person with their identifiers/address/contacts), not org rows, so the patient-app onboarding and chart show a coherent self.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Seed exercises every enriched field (multi-identifier, address hierarchy, contacts, status) so no field is unverified. Replaces patient-app mock-data as the runtime data plane per demo-sample-data rule.
flat_items.license_status: no source code reuse; synthetic non-PHI seed data only.
flat_items.reuse_mode: C (generated scaffold: gen:service-seed)
flat_items.generator_first_target: service-seed-emit (gen:service-seed) for patient-core-service
acceptance: service-seed-emit produces a patients.seed.ts covering enriched fields for >=10 synthetic persons across tenants
seed is idempotent (re-run does not duplicate) and writes through the real repository, not in-memory
no PHI in neutral seed; healthstack overlay seed carries encrypted DOB/SSN-ref for the same person IDs
flat_items.test_plan: Run seed against a real ephemeral PG; assert row counts + idempotency on second run; assert patient-experience read-model returns seeded persons. Generator snapshot test in codegen __tests__ for the emitted seed shape.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Generator-first: fold the enriched-field coverage into service-seed-emit so every service regen inherits it, not a hand-written seed. Removes need for patient-app/src/api/mock-data.ts at runtime.
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: Wire patient index into search-service + adopt clinician patient-search facets and saved cohorts/lists
flat_items.person_centric: Search is a clinician-only management tool; the person never searches others (privacy preserved). The patient-app shows only the person's own record, never a search surface.
flat_items.reshape: Keep search org-side: clinician patient-search panel + saved patient-lists/cohorts/flags. The searched patient sees nothing different. No person-facing search surface created.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Preserve saved-list (openmrs Cohort), patient flags, recent-encounter ordering, and clinical-context filters (ward/clinic) as search facets. ehmp PatientSearchResult field set (icn, mostRecentEncounterDate) adopted.
flat_items.license_status: ehmp/vista permissive (field set safe); openmrs MPL reference-only (Cohort concept only); openhospital GPL reference-only (UI pattern only).
flat_items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) + adopt permissive ehmp field set
flat_items.generator_first_target: na (search-service present-strong; wiring + facet config, no new generator)
acceptance: patient records (enriched) are indexed in search-service per-tenant index on create/update via outbox event
clinician search returns PatientSearchResult fields (name, MRN, icn-equivalent, mostRecentEncounterDate) ranked by recency
saved patient-lists/cohorts persist (Group/Cohort concept) and are clinician-scoped
no person-facing search route added to patient-app
flat_items.test_plan: Integration: index a seeded patient, assert hybrid BM25+vector returns it with the field set; assert facet by ward/flag; assert saved-list create/read scoped to clinician role. Negative test: patient role cannot call search.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-2/3 (real patients to index). search-service already does cross-service hybrid search; this is wiring + facet config, not new infra.
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: FamilyMemberHistory contract + SDK (FHIR R4), generator-first, before service
flat_items.person_centric: Hereditary data is person-owned: the contract supports the patient building their own family tree; the clinician role confirms/curates. RelatedPerson reuses the personal-crm relationship graph to avoid re-keying relatives.
flat_items.reshape: Source FamilyHistoryCPRS is a clinician chart tab. The contract supports a patient-app guided self-entry wizard (person surface) AND a clinician review/attest/CDS-trigger surface (management surface).
flat_items.no_loss_check: Model relative + per-relative diseases/conditions + age-of-onset + vital-status (FHIR FamilyMemberHistory). Maps the CPRS 3-file model (FAMILY HISTORY / RELATIVE / RELATIVE DISEASES, files 500003/500015/500017) field-for-field. Nothing lost.
flat_items.license_status: FamilyHistoryCPRS license UNKNOWN -> legal-review-required: DATA-MODEL SHAPE reference ONLY, no code reuse. openmrs-fhir2 MPL reference-only (RelatedPerson concept). maternity-tracker permissive.
flat_items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only: data model only, NO code)
flat_items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (FamilyMemberHistory.tsp) then sdk-emit, building on @curaos/fhir-client base
acceptance: FamilyMemberHistory TypeSpec covers patient ref, relationship (RelatedPerson), per-relative condition[] (coded), onset-age, deceased/vital-status, note
SDK emits from TypeSpec with no manual edits; fhir-client base types reused (not re-declared)
legal-review note recorded in spec header: data-model-only provenance, FamilyHistoryCPRS license unresolved
flat_items.test_plan: Contract: TypeSpec compiles; SDK emit idempotent. No-loss mapping table in test/doc asserting each CPRS field (500003/500015/500017) maps to a FHIR FamilyMemberHistory element. fhir-client unit test for FamilyMemberHistory resource validation.
flat_items.implementation_notes: healthstack-problems-service is scaffold-only (no src) - greenfield. Contract+SDK FIRST. Hereditary-risk CDS attaches in a later epic. Do NOT copy any FamilyHistoryCPRS code (license unknown).
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: Family-history service slice on healthstack-problems-service (service after contract) + DB-backed seed
flat_items.person_centric: The person owns and authors their family history; clinician attests. Self-entered relatives + conditions persist as real rows, surfaced to both the person (my-family-history) and clinician (review/attest).
flat_items.reshape: Patient-authored entries land for clinician attestation rather than clinician-only entry. Org curation satisfied as a downstream step on the person's self-built tree.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Relative, per-relative diseases/conditions, age-of-onset, vital-status, attestation status all persisted. Clinician attest + link-to-problems retained. Seed is DB-backed real data, not a mock.
flat_items.license_status: data-model-only provenance (see XSRC-E1-5); fresh implementation, no code copied.
flat_items.reuse_mode: E (port-adapt resource shape) + C (generated scaffold for service skeleton/seed)
flat_items.generator_first_target: operation-slice-emit + service-seed-emit against the FamilyMemberHistory TypeSpec

migration + drizzle schema match the contract; relatives and per-relative conditions stored relationally
DB-backed seed creates family histories for seeded persons (links to XSRC-E1-3 patient IDs); idempotent
ConsentGuard applied so family-history reads honor patient consent
flat_items.test_plan: Unit: family-history.service.test for author/attest state machine. Integration (real DB): create relative + conditions, read back via SDK, assert consent-gating denies an unconsented reader. Seed run + idempotency assertion.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Depends on XSRC-E1-5 (contract) and XSRC-E1-9 (ConsentGuard wiring) for read-gating. Reuse RelatedPerson from personal-crm relationship graph where present to avoid re-keying relatives.

flat_items.reshape: Replaces the corpus's org-shaped single-CRUD-screen-per-service default with journey components the ui-app emitter can compose into person-facing flows.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Management surfaces still get DataTable/forms (already present); these are additive person-journey archetypes. No existing ui-kit component removed.

flat_items.license_status: GPL/AGPL UI sources reference-only (pattern, not code). Components authored fresh.
flat_items.reuse_mode: G (pattern-reference-only) + F (fresh build into shared ui-kit)
flat_items.generator_first_target: ui-kit shared components consumed by ui-app-emit/ui-app-files (NOT per-app bespoke)
acceptance: Wizard/Stepper, Entity360/RecordChart, ConsentCard, SearchShell exported from ui-kit index with tokens + a11y (keyboard nav, ARIA)
each has a unit/interaction test (shared-interactions pattern)

flat_items.person_centric: The emitter learns to produce person-first journey surfaces from service contracts, so patient-app (and future apps) get my-chart/my-consents/onboarding as the PRIMARY experience, not a generic org CRUD screen.
flat_items.reshape: Per the gap (ui-app-emit infers ONE generic CRUD screen + single org-shaped surface), extend the emitter so a service flagged person-centric emits journey surfaces (Wizard/Entity360/ConsentCard) bound to its SDK, alongside the existing management surface.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Management CRUD surface still emitted. Persona/journey emission is additive and contract-driven, so no app loses its admin capability. Bespoke screens in patient-app preserved (emit does not clobber hand-authored surfaces).
source_file_refs: gap-analysis.json authored.functional_gaps (ui-app-emit single generic CRUD screen gap)
flat_items.license_status: no source code reuse; emitter logic authored fresh.
flat_items.reuse_mode: generator-evolution (fold archetype emission into ui-app-emit/ui-app-files)
flat_items.generator_first_target: ui-app-emit.ts + ui-app-files.ts (the emitter itself is the change)
acceptance: emitter, given a person-centric service contract, emits person-journey surface files (wizard/entity360/consent) wired to the generated SDK hooks
emitter still emits the management CRUD surface for the same service
re-running emit is idempotent and does NOT overwrite hand-authored bespoke surfaces (preserve-list honored)
snapshot test in codegen __tests__ covers the new emitted surface shapes
