flat_items.title: ConsentGuard wiring + patient-controlled my-consents on healthstack-consent-service (consent-gated reads across clinical services)
flat_items.person_centric: Consent is person-controlled by design: the patient grants/revokes per-resource and per-recipient from patient-app; ConsentGuard enforces it on every clinical service read/write. Inverts the source's admin-set consent boolean.
flat_items.reshape: OpenHospital's single PatientConsensus boolean DTO becomes granular person-owned consent. Person surface = my-consents (grant/revoke per data-category and recipient); compliance surface = consent audit + break-glass-with-reason + GDPR/HIPAA disclosure log.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Granular per-resource+action consent EXCEEDS source. Break-glass (identity-service) + audit + disclosure log preserved. Local is stronger-than-source; no source code imported.
flat_items.license_status: openhospital GPL-3.0 reference-only (concept not code); local consent stronger, nothing imported.
flat_items.reuse_mode: H (reject inbound - local stronger) + wire existing @curaos/healthstack-consent ConsentGuard
flat_items.generator_first_target: na (ConsentGuard pkg present; wire the guard + emit my-consents surface via E1-8 emitter)
acceptance: person can grant/revoke consent per data-category and per-recipient; persisted in healthstack-consent-service
ConsentGuard from @curaos/healthstack-consent gates family-history reads and patient-experience read-model reads by resource+action
break-glass access (identity-service) bypasses consent only with a logged reason captured to audit-core
consent changes emit an event; disclosure log queryable for compliance
flat_items.test_plan: Unit: ConsentGuard allow/deny matrix (resource x action x recipient). Integration (real DB): grant then read succeeds, revoke then read denied (403), break-glass with reason succeeds + audit row written. Assert consent change emits AsyncAPI event.
flat_items.implementation_notes: ConsentGuard pkg already exists (stronger-than-source). This is wiring the guard onto E1-2 (patient-experience) + E1-6 (family-history) read paths and exposing person-controlled grant/revoke. my-consents surface emitted by E1-8.
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: Patient portal tracer-bullet: regenerate patient-app from emitter (my-chart, my-consents, my-family-history, onboarding) on real BFF read-model
flat_items.person_centric: The portal IS the primary person surface: the patient owns and reads their full chart, manages consents, builds family history, and self-registers. The org's record IS the patient's record presented to them.
flat_items.reshape: Portal is the spine, not a bolt-on. One patient-experience read-model backs BOTH clinician chart and patient portal; no parallel PHR store as in OpenEMR.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Attachments (storage/documents-core), flags, lists, secure messaging, AVS-placeholder all wired to existing services. Drifted action names (consent-core-service -> healthstack-consent-service; healthstack-care-plan-service -> healthstack-careplans-service) corrected. Runtime mock-data.ts removed in favor of seeded DB data.
flat_items.license_status: openemr GPL + openmrs-distro AGPL reference-only (feature checklist, no code). maternity/pophealth permissive.
flat_items.reuse_mode: C (generated app via ui-app-emit) on existing read-model + messaging contracts
flat_items.generator_first_target: service-then-app: regenerate patient-app via the E1-8-extended emitter against patient-experience read-model + consent + family-history SDKs
acceptance: patient-app renders my-chart (Entity360 from patient-experience read-model), my-consents (ConsentCard grant/revoke), my-family-history (Wizard), self-registration onboarding (Wizard) using REAL seeded DB data via SDK, no runtime mocks
action filenames match canonical service names (consent-core-service -> healthstack-consent-service; healthstack-care-plan-service -> healthstack-careplans-service)
patient-app typechecks + builds clean; 401 redirects to auth; i18n switcher works
surfaces emitted by generator (E1-8), bespoke screens preserved
flat_items.test_plan: Local JWT + Playwright sweep: log in as a seeded person, assert my-chart shows seeded allergies/problems/family-history, grant/revoke a consent and assert read-gating, complete onboarding wizard and assert a real patient row is created. Typecheck + build. Verify against running app (verify-before-build).
flat_items.implementation_notes: TRACER BULLET that closes the epic: depends on E1-2/3 (real patients), E1-6 (family-history), E1-8 (emitter journey surfaces), E1-9 (consent). patient-app currently scaffold-only (surfaces/AppShell only) and has drifted action names + mock-data.ts. Fix the EMITTER (E1-8), do not hand-edit per-app. Remove mock-data.ts from the runtime data plane per demo-data rule.
flat_items._epic: Healthcare core (registration, demographics, search, portal, consent, family-history)

flat_items.title: Make scheduling.tsp the real contract (appointments/providers/slots/patient-appointments/queue-lanes/desk-metrics) replacing the mold stub
flat_items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
flat_items.license_status: vista-m/RAPTOR Apache-2.0 safe-to-vendor-or-copy; openemr GPL-3.0 + openmrs-distro AGPL-3.0 = reference-only (no code lift). This item authors fresh TS contract from the EXISTING local controller routes; sources inform field-set only.
flat_items.person_centric: person_surface = patient-app book/my-upcoming-visits/check-in/queue-position; management_surface = front-desk grid + provider templates + overbook + desk-metrics/alerts. Same Appointment/Slot/QueueEntry data, two re-centered SDK consumers.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Queue/waiting-room lanes, provider+room scheduling, recurrence, find-next-slot, desk metrics/alerts all expressed as contract models; nothing the source desk grid does is dropped (room+find-next-slot land in XSRC-E2-7).
flat_items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec
acceptance: scheduling.tsp declares typed models + @route ops for EVERY route already in src/schedulings/schedulings.controller.ts: GET / (collection), GET desk-metrics, GET desk-alerts, GET appointments, GET providers, GET patients, GET bookable-providers, GET slots, GET patient-appointments, POST appointments (BookAppointmentInput), PATCH appointments/:id/lane (MoveLaneInput), POST patient-appointments (PatientBookInput), POST :id/advance, PATCH :id (ClinStatusInput); plus the generic admin GET /scheduling list+detail.

Model fields mirror the live Zod DTOs in scheduling.dto.ts (QueueLane enum: scheduled|checked-in|roomed|with-provider|checkout; ClinStatus enum: scheduled|checked-in|roomed|in-progress|complete|no-show) so SDK types == runtime validation.
bunx tsp compile specs/scheduling.tsp emits dist/openapi.yaml with all ops and zero diagnostics; the mold health/protected/whoami probes remain.
No em/en dashes; generator-provenance header preserved.
flat_items.test_plan: spec:openapi compile gate (zero diagnostics); assert dist/openapi.yaml contains paths for /schedulings/appointments, /schedulings/slots, /schedulings/patient-appointments, /schedulings/appointments/{id}/lane, /scheduling (admin list). Contract-vs-controller route parity check (every controller @Get/@Post/@Patch has a tsp op).
flat_items.implementation_notes: The controller + DTOs already encode the full surface; this item lifts that surface into the contract that was never updated past the mold. This is the generator-first prerequisite: no @curaos/scheduling-sdk can exist for these routes until the contract is real. Per [[curaos-generator-evolution-rule]], any model shape that should apply to EVERY service goes in tools/codegen/templates/service-core/specs/, but appointment/slot/queue models are domain-specific so they live in this service tsp.

flat_items.no_loss_check: Every contract op from XSRC-E2-1 yields an SDK method; nothing unreachable.

SDK typechecks and exports request/response types derived from the contract models (QueueLane, ClinStatus enums included).
sdk-emit test asserts the scheduling client is generated identically to the auth-sdk shape (no bespoke hand-edits).

flat_items.title: Add real Appointment/Slot/ProviderSchedule/QueueEntry Drizzle tables + forward migration to scheduling-service
flat_items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
flat_items.license_status: Field set informed by vista-m File 44 APPOINTMENT (Apache-2.0, copyable pattern) + openmrs QueueEntry/QueueRoom (AGPL reference-only). Schema authored fresh; no GPL/AGPL code lifted.
flat_items.person_centric: Tables are the neutral spine both surfaces read. Slot = published bookable window (drives patient self-book); QueueEntry.lane drives the person's queue-position view AND the desk board.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Appointment status lifecycle (scheduled..no-show), provider+slot linkage, queue lane, double-booking-prevention unique index, recurrence reference to calendar-core all present; replaces the in-memory front-desk.read-model.ts seed with no field loss (the seed shape is the column source-of-truth).
flat_items.generator_first_target: service
acceptance: schema.ts adds tables in scheduling_core: appointment (id, tenant_id, patient_ref, provider_ref, slot_id, calendar_event_uid, start_at timestamptz, duration_min, reason, status, lane, created/updated), slot (id, tenant_id, provider_ref, start_at, duration_min, published bool, capacity), provider_schedule (id, tenant_id, provider_ref, template/recurrence ref to calendar-core), queue_entry (id, tenant_id, appointment_id, lane, priority, entered_at).
All timestamps are timestamptz; tenant_id present on every table (Citus shard-key per [[curaos-postgres-rule]]); no PHI columns (patient/provider are references, per AGENTS.md PHI boundary).
Unique index prevents double-booking a provider+slot; lane CHECK constraint matches QueueLaneSchema.
Forward-only migration generated via drizzle-kit; schema.ts == SQL == meta snapshot (the 3-artifact invariant); per [[curaos-rolling-update-rule]] no -v2 parallel path.
Seed migration (XSRC-E2-3 ships skeleton; XSRC-E2-4 fills) keyed to DEMO_TENANT_ID a1f0a1f0-...001 with ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING.
flat_items.test_plan: drizzle-kit generate produces no drift; migration applies clean against a scratch DB; assert double-booking unique index rejects a duplicate provider+slot insert (one runnable check); assert lane CHECK rejects an invalid lane.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Column list MUST be derived from front-desk.read-model.ts + scheduling-admin.read-model.ts so the swap from in-memory seed to repo is field-for-field (the read-model header documents this exact swap-point). Use bun run gen:service-seed to emit the seed skeleton (correct next migration number + journal entry) rather than hand-deriving it.
flat_items._epic: Clinic scheduling (appointment/provider/room scheduling, queue, triage)

flat_items.title: Repo-back the scheduling service: replace in-memory front-desk read-model with Drizzle repo + real demo seed
flat_items.reuse_mode: G:pattern-reference-only
flat_items.license_status: First-party. No external code.
flat_items.person_centric: Patient self-book (POST patient-appointments) and queue-position read resolve against real rows; front-desk board + desk-metrics read the same rows. Both surfaces now show identical persisted truth.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Every method the in-memory read-models expose (adminList, appointments, providers, slots, patientAppointments, deskMetrics, deskAlerts, board lanes) is reimplemented against the repo with the same envelope shapes; no route or RBAC change; no capability dropped.
flat_items.generator_first_target: service
acceptance: A scheduling.repository.ts reads/writes the new tables tenant-scoped (principal.tenantId only); controllers call the repo via the service, not the in-memory maps.

flat_items.no_loss_check: Person sees only their own data; the management capabilities (bed board, full queue, ADT actions) are NOT removed - they live on the management surface (XSRC-E3-10). Person surface is additive, loses no business capability.
flat_items.reuse_mode: E:port-adapt
flat_items.license_status: Generated app from license-clean SDKs. openhospital-ui/openmrs ESM = UX reference only (org-centric, deliberately NOT copied).

acceptance: @curaos/fhir-client exports FileMan<->FHIR datetime conversion + bundle dedup ported verbatim from Apache-2.0 C0FHIR with NOTICE attribution file present
sdk-emit gains a FHIR-R4 translator-pair codepath so a resource declared in a service .tsp emits a typed toFhir/fromFhir pair into that service's generated SDK

flat_items.test_plan: Unit: assert FileMan datetime '3240615.1430' round-trips to ISO and back; assert bundle dedup collapses duplicate entries by fullUrl. Generator snapshot test: a fixture .tsp with one FHIR resource emits a toFhir/fromFhir pair (sdk-emit.test.ts).
flat_items.no_loss_check: Full FHIR-R4 datetime precision + bundle semantics preserved from source utils; no clinical field dropped.
flat_items.implementation_notes: fhir-client is README-only today (verified: 0 src). Copy ONLY the generic permissive utilities verbatim with NOTICE; port resource SHAPES as fresh TS. This unblocks the per-resource contract items by giving them a shared codec + a generator path so each resource is checklist-verifiable from contract.

flat_items.title: Closed-loop critical-result follow-up: event-driven escalation across orders + tasks-core + notify (+ patient next-step)
flat_items.person_centric: Source (AWARE) is a clinician acknowledgement + QI-supervisor system; the person is invisible. Re-center so the person is never the last to know: when a critical result needs action the patient-app delivers a clear next-step instruction (call your doctor / come in) while the clinician escalation chain runs in parallel. Management surface: critical-result inbox, acknowledgement, no-ack escalation chain, who-saw-when audit.
flat_items.reuse_mode: E port-adapt (AWARE VEFA.AlertCache + escalation-timestamp + follow-up-action model); pattern over existing notify+tasks primitives
flat_items.license_status: aware permissive (Apache-2.0 family) = safe-to-vendor port-adapt.

no-ack timer escalates up a configurable chain with escalation timestamps recorded
acknowledgement closes the loop and is audited (who-saw-when)
patient receives a plain-language next-step notification via their chosen channel
event payloads carry references only (no PHI), per the data-boundary rule

flat_items.no_loss_check: Acknowledgement tracking, escalation timestamps/chain, follow-up action prompts, audit all preserved; patient added to the loop without removing clinician escalation.

flat_items.no_loss_check: All cadence semantics already proven in donation-core (frequency, next_due_at, BullMQ trigger, month-end clamp) preserved verbatim; nothing dropped, only generalized so subscription/contract/recurring-invoice reuse one primitive (DRY).

flat_items.test_plan: Unit: month-end clamp vectors (31st across short months, leap year), each frequency interval; assert nextDueAt is deterministic. Reuse donation-core's existing recurrence tests retargeted at the shared helper.

Extract the smallest pure function donation already proved, plus a codegen partial so subscription/contract/recurring-invoice services wire the same BullMQ trigger pattern. flat_items.reuse_mode: MODE E port-adapt (Odoo account_tax / res_currency model+algorithm as fresh TS)
flat_items.license_status: Odoo LGPL-3.0 - port-adapt model+logic only, NO verbatim copy. legal_review=true in reuse ledger.
flat_items.person_centric: Person sees a correct itemized tax line + 'why this tax' tooltip on their invoice/receipt, and amounts in their own preferred currency with a transparent rate-as-of note. All rule/repartition/FX complexity stays on the management config side.
flat_items.no_loss_check: Compound tax, repartition (deductible vs non-deductible), withholding, currency master + rate feed + FX gain/loss balancing entry all preserved as engine capability; jurisdiction localization filed forward per-locale (YAGNI), not dropped.

acceptance: tax-engine computes line tax for: single rate, compound (tax-on-tax), and repartition (deductible/non-deductible split) - matching Odoo account_tax test vectors re-expressed in TS
tax-engine supports withholding (negative/held portion) as a distinct result field
currency converts amounts using a rate-as-of, returns int64 cents, and computes an FX gain/loss delta on settlement vs original booking rate
All money is int64 cents (no float); rounding rule documented and tested at the cent
No verbatim Odoo source lifted - implementation is original TS (license gate)
flat_items.test_plan: Unit money paths with explicit vectors: compound tax order, repartition split sums to gross, withholding portion, FX settlement gain and loss both produce a balancing delta. These are money-path checks (non-negotiable per ponytail).
flat_items.risk: High - financial + jurisdiction correctness; legally binding. Mitigate with strong test vectors per the gap's stated requirement. License: port algorithm only.
flat_items.implementation_notes: These two packages are blocking dependencies for AR invoicing, multi-currency GL, and expense posting. Build them standalone with vectors before any service wiring. Ponytail: do not scaffold per-jurisdiction localization now (gap explicitly defers localization as YAGNI / codegen-emitter follow-up).

price_rule drizzle table + migration; rules are DB-backed (gen:service-seed)
sales quote/cart lines call commerce price resolution and store the resolved PriceResult (price + reason)

Public self-serve sign link works for a non-account counterparty (harvested newonlinesign UX), routed through esign-core's existing signing-store.

Existing apps with valid contracts still generate clean
flat_items.test_plan: ui-app-emit.test.ts: fixture contract missing search -> generating a search-shell throws with the operation name; fixture with search -> generates; record-chart without detail op throws.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Depends on E10-9 archetype vocabulary. ponytail: read the operations already known to the SDK/contract layer (operation-slice-emit already enumerates ops), do not parse .tsp again. Fail-closed, clear message pointing at the contract owner.

flat_items.title: fhir-client translator-pair base package + codegen translator-pair emitter (the reusable interop seam)
flat_items.reuse_mode: A copy-verbatim (vista Apache-2.0 utilities: datetime + bundle-dedup, with NOTICE) + G pattern-reference-only -> E port-adapt (openmrs-fhir2 MPL translator-pair convention, fresh TS interfaces, no code copy)
flat_items.license_status: vista-fhir-codex/fhir-on-vista = Apache-2.0 safe-to-vendor (copy utils with NOTICE); openmrs-fhir2 = MPL-2.0 reference-only (pattern only, write our own TS). NOTICE file mandatory for vendored utils.
flat_items.person_centric: Internal mapping seam serving the person surface: guarantees the person's record exports losslessly to/from FHIR for 'my record' download. Patient-compartment scoping (SMART scopes) baked into the base so person endpoints only ever see their own data.

flat_items.generator_first_target: codegen-emitter (fhir-translator-emit) + fhir-client package
acceptance: fhir-client exports a ToFhir/FromFhir translator-pair interface, reference-translator, datetime converter, and bundle-dedup util (no longer README-only; current README is 44B verified)
NOTICE file attributes the Apache-2.0 vista utilities; openmrs-fhir2 contributes pattern only with zero copied lines
codegen `fhir-translator-emit` scaffolds a translator pair + provider stub per FHIR resource declared in a service tsp
emitter has a snapshot test proving idempotent additive output (never clobbers curated translators)
flat_items.test_plan: Unit: translator round-trip lossless on a sample resource; datetime FileMan<->FHIR conversion table; bundle dedup on duplicate-entry bundle. Emitter: snapshot test of generated translator-pair scaffold; re-run idempotency. License: NOTICE-present CI assertion.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Generator-first: the emitter and base package land BEFORE any per-service FHIR implementation so every FHIR-exposing service inherits conformant mapping. fhir-client is currently a 44B README stub (verified). Reuse the existing codegen emitter registration pattern in src/index.ts (subcommand switch). ponytail: do not invent a generic mapping framework beyond toFhir/fromFhir + reference + datetime + dedup; that is the entire proven surface from the sources.

flat_items.no_loss_check: SHA-256 hash-chain integrity preserved (diff is added inside the already-hashed envelope; existing audit-full-envelope-hash + changedfields-scope tests must stay green). OpenEMR audit_master field-diff + Frappe Version before/after capability now matched without copying GPL/AGPL code. The only documented audit enrich item closed.
acceptance: audit-event.schema.ts.hbs emits an optional changedFields diff (field, before, after) inside the hashed envelope
diff respects FieldPermission mask (E13-1): sensitive field values redacted in person-scoped reads
existing audit hash-chain tests (audit-full-envelope-hash-300, audit-event-schema-changedfields-scope) stay green
regenerating services keeps hash-chain verification passing
flat_items.test_plan: Generator: render audit schema, assert changedFields in envelope and inside hash input. Hash: extend existing audit-full-envelope-hash test to include diff, assert chain still validates. Mask: person-scoped diff redacts a masked field. Regression: existing audit template tests green.
flat_items.implementation_notes: audit-core is stronger-than-source (H reject inbound); field-level-change-diff is explicitly named the ONLY enrich item in both the audit mapping and the reuse-ledger audit entry. Generator-first: the diff must be captured at each service's audit emit (mold schema), not only in audit-core, so the hash covers it. Depends on E13-1 for the mask contract. Small scope - schema field + hash inclusion + mask.
flat_items.risk: Including diff in hash input changes envelope shape; guard with the existing length-prefix hash test (audit-chain-hash-v3-lengthprefix-318) to avoid chain breakage across versions.

This is the acceptance tracer for the whole epic - if green, E13-1..E13-6 + E13-8 compose correctly.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Last in order: this is the vertical-slice tracer bullet that proves all prior items integrate on a real clinical resource (encounter-service exists; encounter.dto.ts already references consent per grep). flat_items.title: Subject-rights export + erasure service consuming existing @curaos/events subject-rights schemas (GDPR Art.15/17/20)
source_file_refs: curaos/backend/packages/events/src/subject-rights.ts (SUBJECT_RIGHTS_REQUESTED/STEP_COMPLETED/EXPORT_READY/ERASURE_REQUESTED/ERASURE_COMPLETED/ERASURE_FAILED events + Zod schemas, NO consumer yet)

Export builds a portable per-subject data package (FHIR Bundle + structured JSON) keyed by subjectRef/subjectType, written to object store, emits SUBJECT_RIGHTS_EXPORT_READY with reference-only payload
Erasure fans out reference-only erasure-requested events to owning services and tracks completion; fails-closed and emits ERASURE_FAILED if any owner does not ack
Art.15 access disclosure pulls from audit-core hash-chain (reuse, do not reimplement)
no PHI in any event payload (reference-only envelope enforced)
flat_items.test_plan: Integration: open a subject-rights request -> assert state machine transitions match event schema -> assert export package built + EXPORT_READY emitted -> assert erasure fan-out + completion tracking -> assert ERASURE_FAILED on missing ack. Validate every emitted event against the existing Zod schemas in subject-rights.ts.
flat_items.reuse_mode: H reuse local (events schemas + audit-core) + generator scaffold; no external source code
flat_items.license_status: first-party (events + audit-core local); no third-party license exposure
flat_items.generator_first_target: contract-typespec (subject-rights.tsp) via emitServiceLive; events schemas already canonical owner
flat_items.person_centric: This IS the person-centric spine of the epic: re-centers org compliance into the person's own 'download / delete my data' journey (patient/customer portal self-service), while the management surface gets the DPO/compliance request queue. Same contract, two surfaces.
flat_items.no_loss_check: All subject-rights event types + state transitions in @curaos/events preserved and now actually driven; Art.15 disclosure (who accessed my data), Art.20 portability export, Art.17 erasure all delivered; audit-core capabilities reused not lost.
flat_items.implementation_notes: Schemas already exist with ZERO consumer - this builds the missing consumer/orchestrator, not new contracts. Do NOT duplicate audit-core hash-chain (ledger mode H local stronger). Erasure must respect PHI boundary (overlay schema owners erase their own).
